formations_blocks: England playing out from the back
Croatia starting strongly with an aggressive press/counter-press
Croatia attacking through corners and direct balls
England seeking to build via wide left runs and right-side breaks
adjustments: England trying to play more calmly from the back after early pressure
Kane dropping deep to link play
Rice taking over corner duties after England's first sustained attack
key_matchups: Perisic vs England's right side on the early corner and left-side advance
Rice intercepting Perisic's low cross
Kane vs Livakovic from the penalty spot
Modric vs Madueke on the loose ball leading to the penalty
phases.summary: Croatia begin the phase on the front foot, forcing England into mistakes when they try to build from deep and creating early danger from a corner and a Perisic-driven attack. England gradually settle through a couple of transitions, then capitalize on a Croatia mistake when Modric is dispossessed/penalizes Madueke in the box, with Kane eventually converting the retaken penalty after Livakovic's save was ruled out for encroachment.

formations_blocks: England attacking in transition after winning the ball in midfield
Croatia pressing/attacking with advanced right-sided delivery and loose-ball pressure
adjustments: Kane dropping deep to link play
Madueke sent in behind on the right
Bellingham carrying attacks from the left
England becoming more dangerous on the break after the penalty
key_matchups: Perisic vs James on Croatia's right-sided cross into the box
Musa vs James on the penalty-area scramble
Madueke vs Croatia's right-side defenders during England's counterattack
phases.summary: This phase starts with Croatia threatening from the right, but England are repeatedly offered routes forward through direct transitions and loose-ball recoveries. After the retaken penalty, England look much more dangerous in open play, with Kane dropping in to connect attacks and runners like Madueke and Bellingham exploiting space, before Croatia briefly level the game and England respond quickly to restore the lead.

adjustments: Anthony Barry says England 'fell back into fearful patterns', becoming 'confused' and 'complicated'
England restarted after the break and immediately continued to threaten from corners
key_matchups: Declan Rice's corners vs Croatia's set-piece organization
O'Riley unmarked at the far post from Rice's corner
phases.summary: England went into half-time leading 2-2 but with clear signs of tactical and emotional instability, as Anthony Barry described them as slipping into fearful, confused, and complicated patterns. The restart brought no personnel changes, yet England quickly created another major set-piece chance when Rice's corner found O’Reilly free at the far post, underlining Croatia’s weakness defending dead balls and England’s continued threat from them.

formations_blocks: Croatia sustained possession and probing attacks after the restart
England pressed and attacked from a set-piece/corner-heavy phase
Croatia appeared to be under sustained pressure and hanging on defensively
adjustments: England immediately increased attacking intensity after halftime, scoring at 47'
Croatia tried to steady the game through Baturina dribbling right and Modric set pieces
England repeatedly targeted the right side through Madueke and James, with Rice delivering multiple corners
key_matchups: Bellingham vs Pasalic
Rice vs Livakovic
O'Reilly vs Livakovic
Gordon vs Livakovic
James vs Modric
phases.summary: The phase begins with Croatia equalizing late in the first half, but England respond instantly after the restart and seize control through Bellingham's quick second goal. From there, England build sustained pressure via right-sided combinations and a stream of corners, while Croatia attempt to slow the momentum with possession, dribbles, and Modric dead balls, but spend much of the phase defending their box. Livakovic is repeatedly tested, including a double save, as Croatia are forced to absorb wave after wave of English pressure.

formations_blocks: England appear to be defending a lead more conservatively after 80', with Spence introduced to shore things up and retain possession
Croatia continue to push forward in search of a comeback, creating late pressure with crosses, shots from range, and attacks through the middle
adjustments: Croatia made a double substitution at 79' with Kramaric and Vlasic replacing Mario Pasalic and Baturina
England substituted Bellingham for Spence at 80', signaling a more defensive approach to protect the lead
Livakovic came off his line to block-clear a danger on 75'
key_matchups: Rashford vs Stanisic on the England left
Pickford vs Marco Pasalic on the right-sided box shot
Pickford vs Matanovic from distance
Pickford vs Kramaric's cross and the second-ball phase involving Sucic
Spence vs Livakovic on the right-side break
Pickford vs Kovacic from range
phases.summary: The phase is defined by Croatia’s late push for an unlikely comeback, with shots, crosses, and half-chances forcing England’s keeper into action while England initially remain dangerous on the break. After Bellingham’s withdrawal for Spence, England shift toward game management, and Rashford’s 85th-minute goal effectively kills the contest after Croatia had briefly threatened.
